lanesets.name: Continents & Oceans
lanesets.description: Earth divided into the seven traditional continents (Europe and Asia counted separately) plus the five named oceans.

lanes.name: Africa
lanes.description: The second-largest continent, south of the Mediterranean and joined to Asia at Suez.

lanes.name: Europe
lanes.description: The continent west of the Urals, Caucasus, and the Bosphorus.

lanes.name: Asia
lanes.description: Earth's largest continent, from the Urals and Suez eastward to the Pacific.

lanes.name: North America
lanes.description: The continent from the Arctic through Central America.

lanes.name: South America
lanes.description: The southern continent of the Americas.

lanes.name: Oceania
lanes.description: Australia, New Zealand, and the Pacific island groups.

lanes.name: Antarctica
lanes.description: The southernmost continent, around the South Pole.

lanes.name: Arctic Ocean
lanes.description: The smallest and shallowest ocean, around the North Pole above roughly 66°N.

lanes.name: Atlantic Ocean
lanes.description: The ocean between the Americas to the west and Europe/Africa to the east.

lanes.name: Indian Ocean
lanes.description: The ocean bounded by Africa, southern Asia, and Australia.

lanes.name: Pacific Ocean
lanes.description: The largest ocean, spanning from the Americas to East Asia and Australia across the antimeridian.

lanes.name: Southern Ocean
lanes.description: The circumpolar ocean surrounding Antarctica below roughly 60°S.

lanesets.name: Landmasses & Oceans
lanesets.description: Earth divided into its major continuous landmasses (Eurasia and the Americas each joined) plus a single combined ocean domain.

lanes.name: Eurasia
lanes.description: The combined European and Asian landmass, the largest on Earth.

lanes.name: Africa
lanes.description: The African continent.

lanes.name: The Americas
lanes.description: North and South America, joined at the Isthmus of Panama.

lanes.name: Australia & Oceania
lanes.description: Australia, New Zealand, and the Pacific island groups.

lanes.name: Antarctica
lanes.description: The southern polar continent.

lanes.name: Oceans
lanes.description: All of the world's oceans and seas as a single domain.

lanesets.name: Global Regions
lanesets.description: A finer division of the continents into world regions (UN geoscheme sub-regions) plus the five named oceans.

lanes.name: Western Europe
lanes.description: The Western Europe world region.

lanes.name: Northern Europe
lanes.description: The Northern Europe world region.

lanes.name: Southern Europe
lanes.description: The Southern Europe world region.

lanes.name: Eastern Europe
lanes.description: The Eastern Europe world region.

lanes.name: Western Asia
lanes.description: The Western Asia world region.

lanes.name: Central Asia
lanes.description: The Central Asia world region.

lanes.name: Southern Asia
lanes.description: The Southern Asia world region.

lanes.name: Eastern Asia
lanes.description: The Eastern Asia world region.

lanes.name: South-Eastern Asia
lanes.description: The South-Eastern Asia world region.

lanes.name: Northern Africa
lanes.description: The Northern Africa world region.

lanes.name: Western Africa
lanes.description: The Western Africa world region.

lanes.name: Eastern Africa
lanes.description: The Eastern Africa world region.

lanes.name: Middle Africa
lanes.description: The Middle Africa world region.

lanes.name: Southern Africa
lanes.description: The Southern Africa world region.

lanes.name: Northern America
lanes.description: The Northern America world region.

lanes.name: Central America
lanes.description: The Central America world region.

lanes.name: Caribbean
lanes.description: The Caribbean world region.

lanes.name: South America
lanes.description: The South America world region.

lanes.name: Australia & New Zealand
lanes.description: The Australia & New Zealand world region.

lanes.name: Melanesia
lanes.description: The Melanesia world region.

lanes.name: Antarctica
lanes.description: The Antarctica world region.

lanes.name: Arctic Ocean
lanes.description: The smallest and shallowest ocean, around the North Pole above roughly 66°N.

lanes.name: Atlantic Ocean
lanes.description: The ocean between the Americas to the west and Europe/Africa to the east.

lanes.name: Indian Ocean
lanes.description: The ocean bounded by Africa, southern Asia, and Australia.

lanes.name: Pacific Ocean
lanes.description: The largest ocean, spanning from the Americas to East Asia and Australia across the antimeridian.

lanes.name: Southern Ocean
lanes.description: The circumpolar ocean surrounding Antarctica below roughly 60°S.

lanesets.name: Cradles of Civilization
lanesets.description: A thematic division: the traditional cradles where early civilizations independently arose, with everywhere else grouped as "Outside the cradles".

lanes.name: Mesopotamia
lanes.description: The Tigris–Euphrates river system of the Fertile Crescent, where Sumerian city-states arose from the 4th millennium BCE.

lanes.name: Nile Valley (Egypt)
lanes.description: The Nile river valley, cradle of Ancient Egyptian civilization from c. 3100 BCE.

lanes.name: Indus Valley
lanes.description: The Indus river basin, home of the Harappan civilization from c. 2600 BCE.

lanes.name: Yellow River (China)
lanes.description: The Yellow River basin of northern China, heartland of early Chinese civilization.

lanes.name: Mesoamerica
lanes.description: The region from central Mexico through Central America, cradle of the Olmec, Maya, and Aztec civilizations.

lanes.name: Andean South America
lanes.description: The central Andes and Peruvian coast, where the Norte Chico/Caral civilization arose c. 3500 BCE.

lanes.name: Outside the Cradles
lanes.description: Everywhere not among the traditional cradles of civilization — the rest of the world's land and water.
